session.name: MPI-8 icons, build hash, and portable artifact implementation

goal.original: Continue MPI-8 by generating platform icon assets from assets/mascot/logo.png, then move to build-hash injection and full portable artifact implementation.
goal.summary: Platform icons, build hash injection, Windows full portable/update staging, built-in archive writers, and local update-from-zip smoke validation are implemented. The work is ready for user verification and real platform launch validation; MPI-8 remains in doing.

completed: Added source default buildInfo.js and portable-stage build hash stamping
Wired error reports to include build.hash and backend build:<hash> labels for valid non-dev hashes
Implemented Windows full portable staging, update-bundle staging, zip archives, and update-from-zip application
Fixed full zip archive layout so full artifacts extract into the CubricVision platform root folder while update bundles remain rootless
Excluded dev-only roots and .env files from portable app staging
Added mechanical Linux/macOS dry-run archive paths and updater templates
Recorded validation evidence in MPI-8 validation.md
pending: User verification of this implementation slice
Launch Windows full portable artifact from start.bat outside the repo
Exercise Linux artifact extraction/launch and engine bootstrap on Ubuntu
Keep macOS marked maintainer-untested until real validation is recorded

task_card.title: Portable distribution, updater, and release validation

completed: MPI-8 plan, checklist, validation notes, and events were updated for this implementation slice.
Coordination file states for this slice were moved to needs_verification.
Strict JSON parse, diff whitespace, syntax checks, dry-run platform staging, full Windows staging/archive, local update smoke, and lint results were recorded.
pending: Current session should be committed after this handoff is written.
After user verification, mark this slice verified and update/release the pending file states.
Do not move MPI-8 to done until final platform validation is represented and explicitly approved.
Ask before updating .claude/rules/ if later validation changes component wiring, workspace routing, events, state, or Comfy injection behavior.

next_action.description: Continue MPI-8 from the recorded validation state: verify the committed icon/build-hash/portable artifact implementation, then launch the Windows portable artifact from start.bat and proceed with Windows platform validation before Linux/macOS validation.

key_decisions: Source/dev runs export BUILD_HASH = 'dev'; scripts/build-portable.mjs overwrites only the staged app copy with the short commit hash.
Error-report backend normalizes build hashes and only adds build:<hash> labels for valid non-dev hex hashes.
Portable archives are generated by scripts/build-portable.mjs without adding a packaging dependency.
Full zip artifacts include the staged root folder; update-bundle zips remain rootless so update/apply-update.cjs can apply manifest-listed paths directly.
Portable app staging deliberately excludes agent state, git metadata, GitHub templates, .env files, tests, lint configs, and build helper scripts.
Windows update-from-zip uses Electron as Node when available, with a shared update/apply-update.cjs helper and rollback folder creation.
Linux/macOS paths are mechanically staged but not platform-validated on real hosts.
constraints: No NSIS, DMG, AppImage, or system-wide installer flow for MPI-8.
Do not require Git for user updates; updater scripts use release assets/manifests.
Do not ask users to manually merge folders; updater scripts own replacement/preservation.
macOS remains maintainer-untested until real validation exists.
Linux generation remains unvalidated unless tested on stronger hardware or by contributors.
MPI-8 remains in doing; this implementation is awaiting user verification.

resume_prompt: Read .agents/mpi-kanban/state/handoffs/daab62ab-9252-480e-9bac-0db65f5eb153.json and use mpi-continue to continue from where we left off. The next action is: Continue MPI-8 from the recorded validation state: verify the committed icon/build-hash/portable artifact implementation, then launch the Windows portable artifact from start.bat and proceed with Windows platform validation before Linux/macOS validation.
